components.text:
I found it buried in a ruined compound under collapsed stone. The first result was ash. The second was unidentifiable. The third was a pill that closed a gash on my forearm in minutes.
I have not moved the cauldron since. Some things are worth building a camp around.

Place the cauldron. Provide fire — the right kind matters more than the amount. Add the herbs in the right combinations. Wait.
Do not open it before it finishes. I learned this early and the injury was not small.
What emerges depends on what you put in. Most batches will teach you something. Not all of them will be pleasant lessons.
